1_executive_summary.goal: Make fresh rescans report zero drift when the code inventory is unchanged so strict CodeRef planning can trust drift.json again.
1_executive_summary.description: Fix the drift generator to compare elements using a stable project-relative path format instead of mixing absolute current paths with relative previous index paths.
1_executive_summary.scope: Update coderef-core drift detection and regression tests, rebuild the package, then verify coderef-workflow no longer reports false 200% drift after populate.

risks: If path normalization changes the report payload but not the identity key, the false added and deleted counts will persist.
If the fix changes real drift semantics, existing drift coverage may regress and hide legitimate added, deleted, or modified elements.
If the built dist output is not regenerated, downstream consumers will continue running the broken drift generator.

current_state: index-generator already writes project-relative file paths into index.json.
drift-generator currently builds current element IDs from raw state.elements, and those elements retain absolute file paths.
coderef-workflow strict preflight correctly rejects drift over 10%, so the false 200% report blocks create-workorder and create-plan until coderef-core is fixed.

features: Normalize current and previous drift comparison elements to the same project-relative path convention.
Return added, deleted, and modified entries using normalized file paths for a stable drift report contract.
Add a regression test covering relative previous index paths versus absolute current scan paths.
Verify the fix by rebuilding coderef-core and repopulating coderef-workflow.

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.ts and src/pipeline/generators/index-generator.ts to confirm where absolute-versus-relative path normalization diverges.
Edit src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.ts so both current scan elements and previous index elements are normalized to project-relative forward-slash paths before identity maps are built.
Edit drift report output generation so added, deleted, and modified file entries also use the normalized project-relative path format.
Add a drift-generator regression test covering unchanged elements where previous index paths are relative and current state paths are absolute.
Run the coderef-core drift generator test file and confirm the new regression plus existing drift behavior both pass.
Rebuild coderef-core so dist/src/pipeline/generators/drift-generator.js reflects the source normalization fix.
Repopulate coderef-workflow and confirm the regenerated drift.json no longer reports false 200% drift for an unchanged scan.
Check that coderef-workflow strict planning preflight accepts the fresh populate and that analyze_project_for_planning can continue past drift validation.

phases.name: Source fix and regression coverage
validation: The drift generator reports 0 changed elements for unchanged scans with mixed absolute and relative source paths.
The existing added, deleted, and modified drift behavior still passes in the test suite.

phases.name: Build and downstream verification
deliverables: Regenerate coderef-workflow .coderef artifacts with corrected drift.json
validation: The rebuilt drift generator emits normalized relative file paths in drift.json entries.
The regenerated coderef-workflow drift report is at or below 10% when the scan is unchanged.
coderef-workflow strict preflight advances past drift validation after the repopulate.

edge_cases: Edge case: previous index stores relative paths while current state holds absolute paths for the same unchanged element.
Edge case: modified elements still report real attribute changes after path normalization.
Edge case: deleted elements remain deleted when an element truly disappears between scans.
Edge case: added elements remain added when a new element truly appears between scans.
Edge case: report payloads use forward-slash relative paths consistently across added, deleted, and modified entries.

criteria: The new drift generator regression test passes and driftPercentage is exactly 0 for the unchanged mixed-path scenario.
The coderef-core drift generator test file passes with 100% of its cases green after the change.
A fresh repopulate of coderef-workflow no longer reports 200% drift for an unchanged scan and stays at or below the 10% strict threshold.
Strict planning in coderef-workflow can proceed past drift preflight without adding any bypass or weakening the threshold.

items: Keep the strict 10% drift gate unchanged.
Do not patch around the bug in coderef-workflow before fixing coderef-core.
Keep path normalization logic local to drift detection and report shaping rather than redesigning the scanner pipeline.
Stop after downstream verification once the corrected drift report is confirmed.
